body:
**Category:** integration
**Automation:** auto
**Depends on:** #7, #42

## Description

Build a cross-cutting correctness and failure-injection harness for Moria's virtual voxel world. The harness must continuously compare optimized production behavior with a deliberately small, slow, obviously correct dense-world reference model. It complements subsystem-specific tests: later optimized stores, feature indexes, queries, mutations, streaming, derived representations, and persistence must plug into this common conformance boundary.

The reference model is test-only and independent of the production sparse store, spatial indexes, dirty-discovery implementation, task scheduler, and persistence codec. Production-path scenarios must enter through the public `moria-world` facade and normal schedules; tests may control clocks, task completion, renderer acknowledgements, and injected I/O failures, but may not replace the production store, index, mutation, invalidation, or persistence logic with fakes.

References: `docs/tdd/overview.md`, `docs/tdd/data-model.md`, `docs/tdd/api.md`, `docs/tdd/systems.md`, `docs/tdd/states.md`, and `docs/tdd/implementation-plan.md`.

## Required coverage

- Dense small-world oracle for procedural base truth, registered voxel features, material/density/state bytes, region boundaries, and sparse edit overlays.
- Differential property tests comparing production and oracle truth after generated sequences of atomic and progressive dig/place operations, overlapping edits, exact reversions, and region-edge operations.
- Metamorphic tests proving results are invariant under worker/thread completion order, hash/insertion order, legal batch partitioning, active versus inactive cache state, eviction/reactivation, and save/load boundaries.
- Cross-boundary tests covering queries, collision samples, feature/dependency discovery, derived-data invalidation, stale revision/token rejection, and terminal reconciliation.
- Fault injection for queue pressure, stale results, interrupted or failed persistence, checksum/identity mismatch, partial task completion, missing/out-of-order renderer acknowledgement, and retry/restart behavior.
- Long deterministic randomized histories followed by exact world comparison, save/load round trip, eviction/reactivation, and regeneration of untouched truth.
- Counterexample shrinking where practical, with every discovered failure preserved as a deterministic seed or checked-in regression fixture.
- Production-scale resource and timing assertions remain in F1/F2; this harness must use the same production code paths and instrumentation so small-world correctness cannot diverge from gate behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The test-only dense reference model shares no sparse-store, feature-index, dirty-discovery, mutation-scheduler, or persistence-codec implementation with production code
- [ ] Generated differential tests compare every authoritative voxel byte and all observable query/feature results after each operation, not only final aggregate counts
- [ ] Metamorphic suites prove equivalent results across scheduling order, batch partitioning, cache residency, eviction/reactivation, and persistence round trips
- [ ] Fault-injection suites prove no stale or partially acknowledged derived state can become authoritative or be reported terminally complete
- [ ] Public-path integration scenarios use the real production store, indexes, schedules, mutation pipeline, invalidation logic, and persistence implementation; only external completion/error boundaries are controllable
- [ ] Random failures are reproducible from recorded seeds, minimized when practical, and promoted to durable regression fixtures
- [ ] A documented single command runs the complete conformance suite locally and in CI, with bounded fast and extended profiles
- [ ] Later subsystem test specifications explicitly identify which conformance adapters and invariants they extend
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass

**Depends on:** #7, #42
title: [integration] Establish virtual-world reference-model conformance harness

**Category:** setup

**Depends on:** M-003, M-008, M-009
**Produces:** `assets/config/curated_manifest.ron`, `crates/moria-world/tests/assets/config_curated_manifest_ron_placeholder.rs`

## Description

Create the format-faithful generated-manifest placeholder only after the exact placeholder region-config and ruin-stamp bytes exist. Compute `parameters_digest` from those canonical inputs at their predeclared stable paths and add only the isolated validation fixture; do not modify the shared stable-ID table. Reference: docs/tdd/assets.md; docs/tdd/data-model.md.

## Inputs

- `M-003` — completed prerequisite contract and stable asset declarations
- `M-008` — exact placeholder `product_one_region.ron` bytes
- `M-009` — exact placeholder `ruin_p1.ron` bytes
- `docs/tdd/assets.md; docs/tdd/data-model.md` — authoritative generated-manifest and digest contracts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The placeholder satisfies canonical generated RON with generated-file comment, sorted features/water/objects/route, and a `parameters_digest` computed from the exact M-008 and M-009 bytes
- [ ] Its isolated asset-schema test resolves the exact predeclared ID/path, makes fallback use observable, and proves changing either digest source invalidates the manifest
- [ ] The issue does not edit shared declaration or registry files, and the stable ID/path and shared-handle role remain immutable for acquisition and wire-in
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass
